jokes.text: # and the chorus goes here / # yeah this is where the chorus goes # / # I didn't have time to write it / # but the chorus goes here
jokes.setup: Criss is singing a romantic Valentine's song for Liz
jokes.punchline: He literally sings about not having time to write the chorus while singing it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy - acknowledging the song's incompleteness within the song itself. Classic 30 Rock absurdist humor with perfect execution.

jokes.text: But you're a lady. Right?
jokes.setup: Liz says she doesn't care about Valentine's Day
jokes.punchline: Criss questions whether she's actually a lady, delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Criss's confusion about gender expectations, delivered with perfect deadpan timing.

jokes.text: What I like to celebrate on February 14th? The 1920 founding of the league of women voters in Chicago, Illinois. Interesting fact: They were supposed to meet on the 13th, but they all got lost, so...
jokes.setup: Liz rejecting Valentine's Day romance
jokes.punchline: She celebrates an obscure political anniversary instead, with an absurd historical detail about getting lost
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz character moment - overly specific, feminist-adjacent, and ending with an absurd historical detail. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Good morning, everybody. I'm hanging out with my friend, John. Fox 5 morning news starts... Look over there. And it starts right now. Oh, boy.
jokes.setup: Criss showing Liz a YouTube video
jokes.punchline: Awkward local TV anchor having a meltdown on air
jokes.explanation: Decent cringe humor but lacks character specificity - could be any random viral video.

jokes.text: Mashed potatoes in a Martini glass, we'll open some of that wine we didn't give the super for Christmas because you decided it would come off as racist.
jokes.setup: Liz describing their planned romantic dinner
jokes.punchline: The wine has baggage from a Christmas racist gift concern
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Liz's tendency to overthink social situations and create awkward scenarios.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm glad we gave him those G.E.D. books instead.
jokes.setup: Continuing the Christmas gift story
jokes.punchline: The 'non-racist' alternative gift is actually more condescending
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing how Liz's attempt to avoid being racist led to something arguably worse. Perfect economy and character insight.

jokes.text: I am 0 for 40 on Valentine's Day.
jokes.setup: Liz warning Criss about Valentine's expectations
jokes.punchline: Baseball statistics applied to romantic failure
jokes.explanation: Good character moment using sports metaphor for romantic failure. Perfectly economical and quotable.

jokes.text: I will waste you! You'll have to go through this old bastard first! Bon Jovi.
jokes.setup: Liz mentioned the Major League soundtrack
jokes.punchline: Random aggressive movie dialogue ending with 'Bon Jovi'
jokes.explanation: Random movie quote that doesn't connect well to the scene. Feels like filler content.

jokes.text: Uh, Liz, you know, my mom did just die. Will you be my Valentine?
jokes.setup: Someone asking Liz to be their Valentine
jokes.punchline: Using their mother's death as emotional leverage
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good structure, but lacks character context to land fully.

jokes.text: I just realized I'm gay.
jokes.setup: Continuation of Valentine's Day rejections
jokes.punchline: Sudden sexual orientation revelation as rejection
jokes.explanation: Quick absurdist beat but feels cheap - using sexual orientation as a punchline without deeper context.

jokes.text: If this is going to be 'unterrible' for the first time ever
jokes.setup: Liz describing her Valentine's expectations
jokes.punchline: Made-up word 'unterrible' showing her low standards
jokes.explanation: Great Liz-specific wordplay showing her pessimistic worldview. 'Unterrible' is perfectly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: Well, you know, for thousands of years Japanese diners have used traditional tatami mats... Whoa, okay, no, no, no.
jokes.setup: Liz complaining about eating off a coffee table
jokes.punchline: Criss starts a cultural explanation, immediately shuts down by Liz's reaction
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics - Criss being overly educational, Liz shutting it down. Nice reaction beat structure.

jokes.text: We, as a couple, are going to Ikea.
jokes.setup: Need for a table by tonight with only one option
jokes.punchline: The ominous declaration of going to Ikea 'as a couple'
jokes.explanation: Great setup for the episode's main plot. The dramatic emphasis on 'as a couple' perfectly foreshadows the relationship testing to come.

jokes.text: Release some energy. / Are we talking about something gross? / My animus has become pent up.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining his extra time without Avery
jokes.punchline: Overly clinical description of sexual frustration
jokes.explanation: Good Jack character moment - using unnecessarily fancy language for basic concepts. Tracy's direct question adds good contrast.

jokes.text: 'mommy-daddy sheet monster times' / I've never 'mommy-daddy sheet monstered' myself.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Jack's sexual frustration
jokes.punchline: Tracy's childish euphemism adopted by Jack with clinical precision
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character contrast - Tracy's childish language filtered through Jack's formal delivery. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: Never? Not even during the Love Boat reunion?
jokes.setup: Jack claiming he's never masturbated
jokes.punchline: Tracy suggesting even the Love Boat reunion wasn't arousing enough
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy absurdism - the specificity of Love Boat reunion as a sexual benchmark is perfectly bizarre.

jokes.text: We're not going to chili's until I hit one.
jokes.setup: Tracy at a batting cage
jokes.punchline: Holding dinner hostage until he hits a baseball
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy character moment showing his childish stubbornness and arbitrary rules.

jokes.text: I would kill to get hit in the crotch by a baseball today.
jokes.setup: Jack's frustration about being alone on Valentine's
jokes.punchline: Preferring physical pain to his current emotional state
jokes.explanation: Great dark humor showing Jack's desperation. The specificity and physical comedy element make it memorable.

jokes.text: Oh, no. I've never had a mother-in-law, but I have seen Everybody loves Raymond. 'Debra, where's the figurine I gave you? Raymond, I gave Debra a figurine.' 'Ma!'
jokes.setup: Jack mentioning his mother-in-law visit
jokes.punchline: Tracy's entire knowledge of mother-in-laws comes from a sitcom, with perfect impersonation
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor with Tracy using sitcom knowledge as life experience. The detailed Raymond impression is perfectly character-specific.

jokes.text: The first Jessup moved to this country in 1760 to avoid an embarrassing regifting incident.
jokes.setup: Jack describing the proper, white Jessup family
jokes.punchline: Their immigration was motivated by a regifting scandal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of WASP pettiness - the idea that regifting was so shameful it required fleeing the country is perfectly absurd and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: But, my lord, I gave you these dueling pistols last yuletide. / Yes, of course. Now, if you'll excuse me.
jokes.setup: Following up on the regifting story
jokes.punchline: Flashback showing the actual embarrassing regifting incident with period dialogue
jokes.explanation: Great visual callback that delivers on the setup. The formal period language makes the petty situation even funnier.

jokes.text: What is she, an Egyptian crocodile? 'Cause she is in 'denial.'
jokes.setup: Jack describing Diana's refusal to acknowledge Avery's situation
jokes.punchline: Elaborate setup for a 'Nile/denial' pun with Egyptian crocodile
jokes.explanation: Weak pun with overly elaborate setup. The crocodile element feels forced just to make the geography work.

jokes.text: I've had two coffees.
jokes.setup: Jack just made a terrible pun
jokes.punchline: Blaming the bad joke on caffeine intake
jokes.explanation: Great self-aware followup to the bad pun. Jack's clinical explanation for his joke quality is perfectly character-specific.

jokes.text: most people think of Valentine's Day as a celebration of the burning of a catholic loudmouth, but it's also about love. And escort prices going way up.
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining Valentine's Day to Hazel
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of escort price inflation alongside historical martyrdom
jokes.explanation: Great Kenneth moment combining his naive historical knowledge with accidentally inappropriate economic observation.

jokes.text: I managed a haunted house upstate. But, as you know, the haunted house industry is a real boys' club.
jokes.setup: Hazel talking about her previous job experience
jokes.punchline: Treating haunted houses like a serious industry with gender discrimination issues
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor applying serious workplace discrimination language to a ridiculous industry.

jokes.text: living at a 24 hour fitness
jokes.setup: Hazel describing her living situation in New York
jokes.punchline: She's literally living at a gym
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Hazel's desperate circumstances in an absurd but economical way.

jokes.text: To test unapproved Japanese medications on. / Swallow. / Good girl. / Now, let me know if all your pubic hair falls out. / Oh, my God! / Oh, no, that's what it's supposed to do. I want to make sure it doesn't cause seizures.
jokes.setup: Kenneth introducing Hazel as the first female page
jokes.punchline: Tracy immediately wanting to use her for dangerous medical experiments, with escalating horrible details
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy character moment with effective escalation. The casual delivery of horrible medical details is perfectly Tracy-like.

jokes.text: Sorry to crash this sausage party... / Unnecessary.
jokes.setup: Jenna apologizing for interrupting the men
jokes.punchline: Kenneth pointing out she's not crashing anything since there's a woman present
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Kenneth's literalism and Jenna's self-centeredness. Clean structure.

jokes.text: Sebastian, the producer of America's kidz got singing, OD'd last night in Guy Fieri's apartment.
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining her emergency
jokes.punchline: The random specific detail of it happening in Guy Fieri's apartment
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist detail. Guy Fieri is a perfect random celebrity to add to a drug overdose story.

jokes.text: Actually, Paula didn't want to do anything for Valentine's, so she's just going out to dinner with her tennis instructor, mark.
jokes.setup: Liz saying Pete should go home to his wife for Valentine's
jokes.punchline: Pete's complete obliviousness to his wife's obvious affair
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pete character moment - his oblivious delivery of obvious infidelity signs. Great character-specific comedy.

jokes.text: No, Paul's been performing on a transvestite cruise for the last month. It's called 'Caribbean queens' for emphasize 'No more love on the run'.
jokes.setup: Someone asking about Jenna's plans
jokes.punchline: Elaborate Billy Ocean song pun for a drag cruise name
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna moment with detailed musical theater knowledge applied to cruise entertainment.

jokes.text: Billy Ocean is furious, and very litigious. But he's powerless as long as they stay in international waters.
jokes.setup: The Billy Ocean cruise pun
jokes.punchline: Treating copyright infringement like piracy that can be avoided in international waters
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation combining legal knowledge with maritime law. The image of Billy Ocean as a legal threat is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Ikea's where I used to go pick up newly-single vulnerable chicks. I've gotten women there two, three points hotter than me. So, you know...fours.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining why Liz and Criss will fight at Ikea
jokes.punchline: His pickup success story reveals he rates himself as a 1-2
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-deprecating humor with perfect misdirection. The setup suggests he's successful, the punchline reveals he's rating himself extremely low.

jokes.text: 'Saint lame-entine's nay'
jokes.setup: Liz expressing disdain for Valentine's Day
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily elaborate pun combining 'lame,' 'Valentine's,' and 'nay'
jokes.explanation: Typical Liz over-complicated wordplay. Shows her tendency to work too hard at being clever.

jokes.text: It's like when Mickey Rourke tested his catapult on me.
jokes.setup: Frank warning about testing relationships too early
jokes.punchline: Bizarre personal anecdote involving Mickey Rourke and medieval weaponry
jokes.explanation: Peak Frank absurdism - the random celebrity, the medieval weapon, delivered as casual life advice. Perfectly bizarre and quotable.

jokes.text: We'll take a pube count, but it doesn't feel worth it.
jokes.setup: Following up on Tracy's Japanese medication experiment
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of monitoring pubic hair loss from the medication
jokes.explanation: Decent callback but relies heavily on grossness factor. Jenna's casual delivery helps but it's still somewhat cheap.

jokes.text: And what race were those mannequins, because their skin was gray. They were like faceless sex aliens.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining why he's hitting golf balls poorly
jokes.punchline: Overthinking the racial classification of gray mannequins as 'faceless sex aliens'
jokes.explanation: Great Jack moment showing his overthinking combined with sexual frustration. 'Faceless sex aliens' is perfectly quotable absurdity.

jokes.text: According to your Christmas letter, Avery is in Asia on business. / You know that she was kidnapped by the north Korean secret police. / Don't be vulgar.
jokes.setup: Diana and Jack discussing Avery's situation
jokes.punchline: Diana treating kidnapping by North Korea as 'vulgar' to mention
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Diana's denial and WASP sensibilities. The understatement is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, but who is this 'we' you keep referring to? Your husband has been dead for 15 years.
jokes.setup: Diana referring to 'we' in decisions
jokes.punchline: Jack bluntly pointing out she's been widowed for 15 years
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with Jack's characteristic directness cutting through Diana's delusions.

jokes.text: Wonderful news. You know our pacing horse, squanto? She's foaling in the spring.
jokes.setup: Jack just mentioned her dead husband
jokes.punchline: Diana completely ignores this by talking about horse breeding
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reaction beat showing Diana's complete avoidance of unpleasant topics. The horse breeding detail is perfectly WASP-y.

jokes.text: extracting an American from North Korea is a, uh... / Oh. / Is a lot more difficult than organizing a round-Robin paddle tennis tournament.
jokes.setup: Jack trying to explain the complexity of the North Korea situation
jokes.punchline: He reduces international diplomacy to country club event planning
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Jack adapting to Diana's world. The comparison to paddle tennis tournaments is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: What about organizing three round-Robin paddle tennis tournaments? / I heard that one of them was a disaster. / Who told you that? Bebe Butler? Oh, she's trying to destroy me.
jokes.setup: Jack's paddle tennis comparison
jokes.punchline: Diana treating multiple tournaments like a serious escalation, then revealing country club drama
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and character work. Diana's priorities are perfectly backwards, and Bebe Butler as a social rival is perfect.

jokes.text: Mein herr, meine frau Avery Jessup... Kim Jong-II vuvuzela Charlize Theron... Boomerang my Sheila back to her Joey, mate.
jokes.setup: Jack describing his previous U.N. attempts
jokes.punchline: Increasingly desperate attempts at different accents and cultural approaches
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Jack's desperation through increasingly ridiculous diplomatic attempts. The vuvuzela reference is perfect.

jokes.text: The U.N. is a useless organization, with a ridiculous army. Robin's egg blue helmets? Great camouflage, if you're invading an easter egg hunt.
jokes.setup: Jack criticizing the U.N.
jokes.punchline: The specific color critique leading to Easter egg hunt invasion
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational humor. The specific color analysis leading to the Easter egg hunt scenario is perfectly constructed.

jokes.text: First of all, why do you want flies?
jokes.setup: Diana says 'you catch more flies with honey'
jokes.punchline: Jack's literal interpretation of the idiom
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's logical mind applied to idioms. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: Strewth, what a bull-dingered Valentine's Day. / Oh, and Jack? I speak Australian.
jokes.setup: Jack just tried speaking in an Australian accent to the U.N.
jokes.punchline: Diana revealing she understood his terrible accent attempt
jokes.explanation: Good callback and character revelation. Diana's casual competence contrasts with Jack's desperate attempts.

jokes.text: Unless you count that time when the goose chased you into the Central Park boat pond.
jokes.setup: Criss saying they've never been on a boat together
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of Liz being chased by a goose into water
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Liz's tendency toward physical comedy mishaps. Delivered casually which makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I like myself. I have good taste in drapes. / I wish I'd died on Iwo Jima and never met you. / Whoa. What was that? / Not us.
jokes.setup: Criss being optimistic about Ikea shopping
jokes.punchline: Other couples fighting violently in the background, followed by Liz's quick clarification
jokes.explanation: Great setup for the Ikea relationship testing theme. The extreme escalation to WWII death wish is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I just got my period. / I swear to God, I will stab you!
jokes.setup: Continuing the background couple fighting
jokes.punchline: Increasingly personal and violent threats
jokes.explanation: Weak escalation that relies on shock value rather than cleverness. The period reference feels cheap.

jokes.text: So tense. Just like my mom's back.
jokes.setup: Witnessing the violent couple fighting
jokes.punchline: Criss's calm comparison to his mother's back tension
jokes.explanation: Good Criss character moment showing his laid-back nature contrasted with the chaos around him. Perfect understatement.

jokes.text: In 48 years, I haven't had one good Valentine's. / Man, if I weren't with Lynn now we could team up. Valentine's Day is the perfect time to meet vulnerable women. It's scumbag Christmas!
jokes.setup: Tracy lamenting being alone on Valentine's
jokes.punchline: Frank's enthusiastic description of Valentine's as 'scumbag Christmas'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor. 'Scumbag Christmas' is an instantly quotable phrase that perfectly captures the predatory dating mindset.

jokes.text: Look, guys like us have to pick low-hanging fruit. We look like Far side drawings.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining their dating strategy
jokes.punchline: Comparing themselves to Gary Larson cartoon characters
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware humor. The Far Side comparison is visually perfect and brutally honest about their appearance.

jokes.text: Cat shelters, blood banks, the ice cream stand at a Celtic woman concert... And we strike.
jokes.setup: Frank listing places to find vulnerable women
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and pathetic locations, ending with Celtic Woman concert
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with specific, sad locations. Celtic Woman concert is a perfectly pathetic detail.

jokes.text: Another good place is the beauty salon, where white girls try to do black hair. Sisters come out crying, feeling all bad about themselves, and we walk up on them all, 'Hey, baby, what's wrong?'
jokes.setup: Frank continuing his predatory dating advice
jokes.punchline: Very specific scenario involving hair salon failures and the catchphrase delivery
jokes.explanation: Specific observational humor that sets up the episode's catchphrase. The scenario is detailed and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Then after we watch Fievel goes west, we're gonna get you some action.
jokes.setup: Frank promising Tracy an adventure
jokes.punchline: The 'adventure' starts with a children's animated movie
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Frank's childish priorities. The juxtaposition of kids movie with dating mission works well.

jokes.text: It's not some rinky-dink late night show that a bald idiot produces.
jokes.setup: Jenna comparing her live performance to other shows
jokes.punchline: Insulting Pete directly to his face about TGS
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna character moment showing her casual cruelty and self-importance. The directness of the insult works.

jokes.text: Jenna Maroney's singing live, and all of America is just waiting for that [bleep] To get [bleep] over and over and over again with a [bleep].
jokes.setup: TV promo for Jenna's live performance
jokes.punchline: Increasingly aggressive bleeping that clearly isn't covering singing terminology
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke using censorship bleeps to suggest something much worse than what's probably being said.

jokes.text: if tonight isn't a huge 'jiumph', which is short for 'Jenna triumph'
jokes.setup: Jenna describing her expectations
jokes.punchline: Made-up portmanteau that's unnecessarily complicated
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna character moment. Creating a word that combines her name with triumph is exactly her brand of narcissistic wordplay.

jokes.text: I attacked Nancy Kerrigan.
jokes.setup: Jenna talking about shifting blame like celebrities
jokes.punchline: Casual confession to the famous figure skating attack
jokes.explanation: Brilliant throwaway line. Jenna casually claiming responsibility for one of the most famous sports scandals is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: And I also wish you, a 'bear-y' happy Valentine's Day.
jokes.setup: UN official greeting Jack and Diana
jokes.punchline: Terrible bear pun delivered with diplomatic seriousness
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that's intentionally bad but doesn't land effectively. The cringe factor isn't strong enough to make it work.

jokes.text: Do you see a 14 on my watch? / Yes, it's the date.
jokes.setup: Jack complaining about 24-hour time format
jokes.punchline: The official pointing out there literally is a 14 on his watch
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke. Jack's complaint gets perfectly deflated by literal observation.

jokes.text: New Jersey. I'm just a weirdo.
jokes.setup: Jack asking what country he's from
jokes.punchline: Admitting he's just from New Jersey and is weird
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective self-deprecating humor. The honesty is disarming and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: We both married much, much older men, which can be hard, or, more often, flaccid.
jokes.setup: Diana finding common ground with Jack
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo about older husbands delivered with WASP understatement
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Diana's repressed sexuality emerging through wordplay. The clinical delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: 'Maybe we sit quietly and read our, uh, soccer magazine.'
jokes.setup: Jack suggesting quiet activity
jokes.punchline: Only reading material available is soccer magazine in America
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about American sports preferences.

jokes.text: Three feet, two feet, it says he's right on top of us. / Oh! / Oh, my God, my face. / Oh! Oh! / He's mating with my mouth.
jokes.setup: Kenneth tracking Tracy's escaped lizard with GPS
jokes.punchline: The lizard attacks Hazel's face in a sexual manner
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that relies on gross-out factor. The escalation works but it's somewhat cheap humor.

jokes.text: Go to a strip club on Father's Day, and shout things like, 'I'm proud of you!'
jokes.setup: Frank continuing his predatory dating advice
jokes.punchline: Combining strip clubs with inappropriate paternal affirmation
jokes.explanation: Dark and brilliant humor combining inappropriate venues with wholesome parental support. Perfectly Frank-like twisted logic.

jokes.text: I would go to the swimsuit section and switch the tags. I'd take a size six and put a size twelve sticker on it. And then some thick girl would try to squeeze into it, and come out of the dressing room like, 'I've gotten bigger. Why do I even bother dieting?' And I'm all like, 'Hey, baby, what's wrong?'
jokes.setup: Frank describing his past job at a clothing store
jokes.punchline: Elaborate scheme to create vulnerable women by sabotaging clothing sizes
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark character moment showing Frank's manipulative nature. The detailed scheme and catchphrase delivery are perfectly crafted.

jokes.text: I did 'watchers' to stay pageant-fit, but it was too much math for a six-year-old.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Weight Watchers
jokes.punchline: Hazel was doing Weight Watchers as a small child for pageants
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about child pageant culture. The casual delivery of child dieting is effectively disturbing.

jokes.text: Thank God I found cigarettes.
jokes.setup: Hazel talking about Weight Watchers being too hard
jokes.punchline: Cigarettes as the solution for a six-year-old's weight management
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark escalation. The matter-of-fact delivery of child smoking for pageant weight control is perfectly disturbing.

jokes.text: Oh, baby, did someone throw cereal on you? Let's get you out of these wet clothes. / Oh, what is wrong with you people? / Whatever. Jeremy says she's a tease anyway.
jokes.setup: Frank demonstrating his pickup technique by throwing cereal
jokes.punchline: The woman's outrage followed by Tracy's lizard's opinion
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy setup that pays off the pickup line setup, but the execution feels somewhat cheap. Jeremy the lizard's opinion adds a good absurdist element.

jokes.text: Look, salt and pepper shakers. You put them together, it makes a heart. Take them apart, two red sperms.
jokes.setup: Criss showing Liz romantic items he found
jokes.punchline: The heart-shaped shakers look like sperm when separated
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor about product design. The romantic/crude contrast works for the character dynamic.

jokes.text: What's with the 'neggy' vibes, jeez.
jokes.setup: Liz being negative about his purchases
jokes.punchline: Criss's made-up word 'neggy' for negative
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Criss's laid-back speech patterns. The made-up word feels natural for him.

jokes.text: First things first. I am from Transylvania, but I am not a vampire. I'm just a night owl with a terrible garlic allergy.
jokes.setup: UN official introducing himself
jokes.punchline: Pre-emptively addressing vampire assumptions with logical explanations
jokes.explanation: Good character introduction that addresses obvious assumptions. The logical vampire explanations are well-crafted.

jokes.text: So, this is regarding that boy who went missing in my castle?
jokes.setup: Official trying to understand their visit
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning a missing boy in his castle
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor that plays on vampire tropes while maintaining he's not a vampire. The casual delivery sells it.

jokes.text: Well that is just the beginning, you...penis!
jokes.setup: Diana getting frustrated with the UN official
jokes.punchline: Proper WASP lady calling someone a penis
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Diana's frustration breaking through her proper facade. The word choice is perfectly inappropriate for her character.

jokes.text: This isn't some idiotic civil war in some country without oil. This is my favorite daughter. And she is a beautiful white American.
jokes.setup: Diana escalating her demands
jokes.punchline: Revealing her racist and classist worldview while ranking her daughters
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work showing Diana's horrible worldview. The casual racism and daughter ranking are perfectly awful.

jokes.text: I can see to it that Transylvania never sees another episode of Friends. / No! Monica and Chandler just slept together in London.
jokes.setup: Jack threatening the official
jokes.punchline: Using Friends episodes as diplomatic leverage, official's devastation at spoilers
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation using TV shows as international leverage. The Friends spoiler as diplomatic weapon is brilliantly absurd.

jokes.text: Every time he says he's not hungry when we collect money for pizza, and then he eats, like, three slices.
jokes.setup: Official complaining about Bono
jokes.punchline: Very specific and petty complaint about pizza sharing
jokes.explanation: Great specific complaint that humanizes international diplomacy. The petty pizza politics are perfectly relatable.

jokes.text: at Monica and Chandler's wedding, Rachel's the one who is pregnant.
jokes.setup: Jack continuing to threaten with spoilers
jokes.punchline: Delivering another devastating Friends spoiler as parting shot
jokes.explanation: Good callback escalation. Jack's casual delivery of major plot spoilers as diplomatic weapons is perfectly petty.

jokes.text: I don't know if I'm feeling it, you know? / What about this idea? I know a guy who cuts glass... / No. I took measurements, I looked at colors, we came here. This is the plan.
jokes.setup: Criss and Liz looking at the table they came for
jokes.punchline: Criss immediately wanting to change plans, Liz's rigid adherence to the plan
jokes.explanation: Good relationship dynamics showing their different approaches. Sets up the relationship metaphor perfectly.

jokes.text: We are not letting this table be a metaphor for our relationship. That's what Ikea wants us to do. / I'm just not sure my chair wants to be with this table. / Why, because deep down your chair would rather be with other chairs?
jokes.setup: Liz trying to prevent relationship metaphors
jokes.punchline: Immediately falling into using furniture as relationship metaphors
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy about relationship clichés while immediately succumbing to them. Perfect self-aware writing.

jokes.text: Well, it's just the table thinks the chair takes too many camping trips with Richard.
jokes.setup: The furniture metaphor conversation
jokes.punchline: Liz getting very specific with real relationship concerns through furniture
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of the metaphor with specific relationship details. Shows real concerns emerging through the absurd framework.

jokes.text: 'No, you were wonderful. The way your eyes went dead, just like Avery's.'
jokes.setup: Jack complimenting Diana's aggressive behavior
jokes.punchline: Comparing mother-in-law's dead eyes to wife's as compliment
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy where Jack finds dead eyes romantically appealing in both generations.

jokes.text: In 1978, I was in Playboy, just wearing a necktie and holding hands with a black girl.
jokes.setup: Diana saying Jack doesn't know things about her
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of scandalous past with racist overtones
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation showing Diana's past while maintaining her casual racism. The specific details are perfectly crafted.

jokes.text: Those incredible cheekbones, like an evil Disney queen.
jokes.setup: Jack realizing Diana is like Avery
jokes.punchline: Comparing her to an evil Disney character as a compliment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's attraction through his specific reference points. Evil Disney queen as beauty standard is perfect.

jokes.text: You are not some liver-spotted old man constantly spilling hot tea in his lap until his whole crotch was useless. Useless!
jokes.setup: Diana apologizing for comparing Jack to her husband
jokes.punchline: Very specific and gross details about her dead husband's incontinence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Diana's repressed frustrations emerging. The specific details and repeated 'useless' work well.

jokes.text: Your hair, like a lion's mane. My eyes, like two pools of ice water. / The bats are long and hard. / The gloves are girls. / Balls! / Jeter's thighs in those pants.
jokes.setup: Sexual tension building between Diana and Jack
jokes.punchline: Desperately talking about baseball with increasingly sexual language
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing their attempt to avoid sexual tension by talking about baseball, which becomes equally sexual. Perfect structure.

jokes.text: You know, it's funny. If those teeth were in your vagina, you'd be considered a monster.
jokes.setup: Tracy looking at the lizard that attacked Hazel
jokes.punchline: Bizarre observation about teeth placement and social perception
jokes.explanation: Peak Tracy absurdism. The completely bizarre anatomical observation delivered matter-of-factly is perfectly strange.

jokes.text: Unfortunately, there's no field of medicine that deals with the brain.
jokes.setup: Doctor explaining Jenna's psychosomatic issues
jokes.punchline: Claiming neurology/psychiatry doesn't exist
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist medical humor. The deadpan denial of basic medical fields is effectively bizarre.

jokes.text: 'Unfortunately, there's no field of medicine that deals with the brain. But, I can give you a pamphlet for a cult.'
jokes.setup: Jenna's psychological singing problems
jokes.punchline: Medicine admitting ignorance about brain, offering cult instead
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist medical logic, though could be from any quack doctor character.

jokes.text: Pressure is trying to pass for four when you just turned seven, at the 'Miss Toddler Panama city' pageant. You're crammed into the same five-inch heels you wore the year before, blood pooling in your toes.
jokes.setup: Hazel explaining what real pressure is
jokes.punchline: Horrific details about child pageant abuse delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark character work. The specific horrific details delivered casually create effective disturbing humor.

jokes.text: Pressure is performing on a party boat that catches on fire, your throat burning from the smoke. You still sing so beautifully that it calms the passengers, so that you and the crew can escape.
jokes.setup: Hazel continuing her pressure examples
jokes.punchline: Increasingly dramatic scenarios ending with heroic singing during maritime disaster
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with cinematic disaster scenario. The image of calming passengers with beautiful singing is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Is singing the Yemeni national anthem while a handsome but ruthless general pushes a scimitar into your neck, Kristin Chenoweth's corpse at your feet.
jokes.setup: Hazel's final pressure example
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific international incident with Broadway star murder
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation to completely absurd international incident. The specificity of Yemen, scimitar, and Kristin Chenoweth's corpse is perfect.

jokes.text: This is why you don't just choose one option. / No, this is why you don't waste 45 minutes wandering around the 'Valentine's marketplace.'
jokes.setup: The table they wanted is sold out
jokes.punchline: Each blaming the other with their characteristic approaches
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast showing their different problem-solving approaches leading to mutual blame.

jokes.text: Valentine's is the worst! It's even worse than earth day. You just see so many dirty feet in sandals.
jokes.setup: Liz getting frustrated with Valentine's
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to Earth Day because of foot hygiene issues
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz character moment. The specific complaint about dirty feet in sandals as a holiday critique is exactly her brand of neurosis.

jokes.text: Well what race am I supposed to check? We all come from Africa.
jokes.setup: Liz criticizing him for not finishing his census
jokes.punchline: Philosophical approach to racial categorization
jokes.explanation: Good Criss character moment showing his laid-back philosophical approach to bureaucracy. The reasoning is sound but impractical.

jokes.text: That's why I get out of the nidurgangur in the morning.
jokes.setup: Liz and Criss breaking up dramatically
jokes.punchline: Ikea employee with made-up Swedish furniture name
jokes.explanation: Decent Ikea furniture name joke that provides a small comedic beat during the dramatic moment.

jokes.text: Last night, I dreamed a baby ate my hair.
jokes.setup: Hazel talking about having dreams
jokes.punchline: Completely bizarre and specific dream content
jokes.explanation: Great Hazel character moment with perfectly bizarre dream logic. The specific image is memorable and strange.

jokes.text: These days, it's just a boom box and a guy in a crab costume. He gets shot out of a Cannon at Ravens Games.
jokes.setup: Diana talking about supporting the Baltimore Philharmonic
jokes.punchline: The 'orchestra' is now just a guy in a crab costume with a cannon
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Baltimore's decline through cultural institution decay. The crab costume cannon is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: My taxes are paying for your healthcare! Do a sit-up!
jokes.setup: Jack getting frustrated in the park
jokes.punchline: Yelling conservative talking points at strangers
jokes.explanation: Good Jack character moment showing his political views emerging under stress. The specific healthcare/fitness connection is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Yesterday, I ate all the cheese out of the mouse traps.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining his rough week
jokes.punchline: Eating cheese from mouse traps as a meal
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy character moment showing his bizarre approach to nutrition. The image is both pathetic and absurd.

jokes.text: You're gonna tell me about black lady hair, you 'Louie Anderson' little bitch?
jokes.setup: Frank trying his pickup line on the wrong woman
jokes.punchline: Aggressive response with specific comedian comparison
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Frank's predatory scheme with specific insult. Louie Anderson comparison is oddly specific and effective.

jokes.text: Peter Hornberger, a shoo-in for this Olympic squad, about to arch. You're going to want to watch this, folks, so put down your Rubik's cube, turn off that Yakov Smirnoff standup tape, and set all your swatches to right now.
jokes.setup: Pete's archery flashback
jokes.punchline: Perfect 80s references for what people would be doing instead of watching
jokes.explanation: Excellent period-specific references. Rubik's cube, Yakov Smirnoff, and Swatch watches perfectly capture the 80s zeitgeist.
